mechanism_narrative: A 4-month increase in industrial production signals a strengthening demand environment that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips during a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 4% signals a tightening labor market that compresses wage growth and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips during a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in the federal funds rate below 3% signals a monetary easing cycle that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips during a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 1.4 million signals a strengthening supply environment that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening supply periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips during a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in real GDP above 2% signals a strengthening demand environment that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips during a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in initial jobless claims below 200k signals a tightening labor market that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips during a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in real disposable personal income above 2% signals a strengthening demand environment that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips during a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate below 3% signals a monetary easing cycle that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days, but only during a recession.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in easing periods during a recession are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips during a late-cycle expansion.
